_description: Phase 3 Plan 01 Task 2: Lift preservation geometric argument (COROT-03)
_assert_convention: unit_system=SI, co_rotation_fraction=f in [0,1], v_water_v=0 (no vertical entrainment)

velocity_decomposition.v_water_horizontal_ms: f * v_tan  [horizontal co-rotation only; f in [0,1]]
velocity_decomposition.v_water_vertical_note: No vertical water entrainment by horizontal rotation; water recirculates in the tank horizontally only.
velocity_decomposition.v_rel_vertical_ms: v_loop = 3.7137 m/s  [UNCHANGED for all f in [0,1]]
velocity_decomposition.v_rel_vertical_independence_proof: v_rel_v = v_vessel_v - v_water_v = v_loop - 0 = v_loop. This holds for ALL f because v_water_v = 0 identically (horizontal co-rotation has no vertical component). Therefore v_rel_v is independent of f.

lift_preservation_argument: v_rel_v = v_loop = 3.7137 m/s > 0 for all f. Lift L = 0.5*rho_w*C_L*A_foil*v_rel^2 where v_rel = sqrt(v_rel_h^2 + v_rel_v^2) >= v_loop > 0. Therefore L > 0 for all f < 1 (even if v_rel_h -> 0, v_rel >= v_loop > 0). The stall constraint (AoA limit) restricts f, not the lift formula.

numerical_demonstration.description: v_rel_v = v_loop at multiple f values confirms independence

lambda_eff_physical_meaning: lambda_eff = v_tan / v_rel_h = v_tan / [v_tan*(1-f)] = 1/(1-f) * lambda_design. As f increases, lambda_eff increases. At f=f_stall, lambda_eff = lambda_max = 1.2748.

f_stall_calculation.f_stall_note: f_stall = 1 - 0.9/1.2748 = 0.294003; consistent with plan reference 0.291 (difference 0.0030 due to lambda interpolation vs 1.27)

lift_preservation_verdict: PRESERVED: v_rel_v = v_loop = 3.7137 m/s independent of f. Hydrofoil lift is nonzero for all f < f_stall = 0.2940.
stall_implication: Stall at f_stall = 0.2940 limits useful co-rotation range. Above f_stall, lambda_eff > lambda_max = 1.2748 and ascending foil AoA exceeds stall angle (~15 deg). The stall is not a lift-going-to-zero event; it is an AoA-exceeding-stall-angle event.

f_stall_vs_lambda_eff_max.note: lambda_eff(f_stall) should equal lambda_max by definition of f_stall
